text.title: api-openapi-spec Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-openapi-spec as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent adds new schemas instead of $ref-ing existing ones, creating two diverging definitions of User.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duplicate schemas validate differently for the same entity; generated SDK types produce two incompatible types for the same concept.

failure-mode.description: LLM emits JSON Schema keywords that do not exist in OpenAPI 3.1 (format: phone-number, format: ssn, format: credit-card).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unknown formats pass through linting but break generator type mapping; spectral catches this only if the oas3-schema rule is set to error.

failure-mode.description: Agent defaults to openapi: 3.0.0 in new files; edits preserve 3.0 even in a 3.1 project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OpenAPI 3.0 and 3.1 have different JSON Schema dialects; mixing versions in one project breaks tooling that treats them uniformly.

failure-mode.description: Agent regenerates server stubs and overwrites hand-written service class implementations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generated code overwrites real business logic; .openapi-generator-ignore must protect service/implementation files from regen.

failure-mode.description: Breaking changes auto-merged by CI without human approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Breaking changes cause consumer SDK failures on next deploy; every breaking change requires human sign-off and a semver-major bump.

failure-mode.description: LLM-generated examples contain real-looking PII (email addresses, phone numbers, SSNs).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Realistic examples in public docs expose PII-like strings; use clearly fake data (user@example.com, 555-0100).
